title: Present continuous for future plans
description: Use the present continuous tense to talk about fixed, arranged future plans.

content.title: Present continuous for the future
content.body:
We can use the present continuous (am/is/are + -ing) to talk about FIXED future plans â€” things that are already arranged and confirmed.

Examples:
â€¢ I'm meeting Priya at 6 PM tonight. (already arranged)
â€¢ We're flying to Delhi on Saturday. (tickets booked)
â€¢ She's starting her new job on Monday. (confirmed plan)

Key signals: future time words â†’ tonight, tomorrow, next week, on Friday, in the morning
content.grammar_note:
am/is/are + verb-ing + future time expression
This tense = fixed plan, not a prediction

content.title: Negative and question forms
content.body:
Negative: am/is/are NOT + -ing
â€¢ I'm not coming to the party â€” I have an exam.
â€¢ She isn't attending the meeting tomorrow.

Questions: Am/Is/Are + subject + -ing?
â€¢ Are you coming to Meera's wedding?
â€¢ What are you doing this weekend?
â€¢ Is he travelling to Bangalore next week?

Short answers:
â€¢ Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.
â€¢ Yes, she is. / No, she isn't.
content.grammar_note:
Question: Are you + verb-ing?
Negative: I'm not + verb-ing

vocabulary.meaning: to plan and organise something in advance
vocabulary.example: We've arranged a team lunch for Friday.

vocabulary.meaning: to make a plan definite and official
vocabulary.example: Can you confirm if you're attending?

vocabulary.meaning: a plan of when things will happen
vocabulary.example: Check the schedule for tomorrow's meetings.

vocabulary.meaning: a fixed time to meet someone
vocabulary.example: I have a dentist appointment on Thursday.

vocabulary.meaning: the place where an event happens
vocabulary.example: The venue for the party is Hotel Raj.

vocabulary.meaning: free and able to do something
vocabulary.example: Are you available on Saturday evening?

sentences: I'm meeting my cousin at the airport tomorrow morning.
We're having a team dinner on Friday evening.
She isn't coming to college next week â€” she's travelling.
Are you doing anything special this weekend?
They're moving to their new flat on the 15th.

content.question: Ravi has booked train tickets to Mysore for Saturday. Which sentence is correct?
options: Ravi travels to Mysore on Saturday.
Ravi is travelling to Mysore on Saturday.
Ravi will travels to Mysore on Saturday.
Ravi travel to Mysore on Saturday.
content.correct: Ravi is travelling to Mysore on Saturday.
content.explanation: A confirmed, booked plan uses present continuous: IS + travelling.

content.question: Priya wants to ask her friend about Saturday plans. Which question is correct?
options: Do you come to the wedding on Saturday?
Are you coming to the wedding on Saturday?
Will you coming to the wedding on Saturday?
You are coming to the wedding on Saturday?
content.correct: Are you coming to the wedding on Saturday?
content.explanation: Future arrangement question: Are + you + verb-ing?

content.question: Arjun's calendar shows 'Doctor - 4 PM Tuesday'. Which is correct?
options: Arjun sees the doctor on Tuesday.
Arjun is seeing the doctor at 4 PM on Tuesday.
Arjun will saw the doctor on Tuesday.
Arjun seeing the doctor at 4 PM Tuesday.
content.correct: Arjun is seeing the doctor at 4 PM on Tuesday.
content.explanation: A booked appointment = fixed future plan â†’ present continuous.

content.question: Which sentence uses present continuous for the FUTURE correctly?
options: I am eating breakfast right now.
She is presenting at the conference next month.
They are watching a movie at the moment.
He is cooking dinner currently.
content.correct: She is presenting at the conference next month.
content.explanation: 'Next month' is a future time signal â€” this is a fixed plan. The others describe actions happening now.

content.instruction: Complete with the present continuous form for future plans.

sentences.text: We ___ (have) a family reunion next Sunday. All relatives are coming.
I ___ (not/work) on Monday â€” it's a public holiday.
Meera ___ (join) the new Bangalore office next month.
___ you ___ (attend) the product launch event tomorrow? (Are/attend)

content.instruction: Fill the gap â€” present continuous for future.

sentences.text: Kavya and Sonal ___ (meet) for lunch at 1 PM tomorrow.
The company ___ (launch) its new app next Thursday.
I ___ (not/travel) to Mumbai this weekend â€” plans changed.

content.instruction: Arrange the words to make a correct future plan sentence.
content.answer: I'm having dinner with my friends tonight.
Are you doing anything special this weekend?

content.instruction: Match each situation to the correct present continuous future sentence.

pairs.left: Tickets booked to Goa â€” next Friday
pairs.right: I'm flying to Goa next Friday.
She's seeing the doctor at 3 on Wednesday.

pairs.left: Team call â€” 10 AM tomorrow, in calendar
pairs.right: We're having a team call at 10 tomorrow.

pairs.left: No office â€” public holiday Monday
pairs.right: I'm not going to the office on Monday.
He's attending an interview on Thursday morning.

content.title: Weekend plans â€” friends in Bangalore
content.setting: Kavya and Sonal are colleagues in Bangalore catching up about their weekend.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Sonal, do you have any plans this weekend?

dialogue.speaker: Sonal
dialogue.text: Yes! I'm attending my cousin's engagement on Saturday. The function is in Koramangala.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Oh wow, how exciting! Are you going alone?

dialogue.speaker: Sonal
dialogue.text: No, my whole family is coming. My parents are arriving from Pune on Friday night.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: That's lovely. What are you doing on Sunday?

dialogue.speaker: Sonal
dialogue.text: I'm resting at home in the morning, and then I'm meeting a friend from college for coffee in the evening.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Sounds like a great weekend! I'm visiting the new exhibition at Cubbon Park on Sunday.

dialogue.speaker: Sonal
dialogue.text: Oh nice! Are you going with anyone?

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Yes, I'm going with my neighbour Meera. We've been planning it for two weeks â€” we already have the tickets.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: We're meeting there at 11 AM. Are you working late tonight? We could have chai before leaving.

dialogue.speaker: Sonal
dialogue.text: I'm finishing at 6 today. Yes, let's do that!

comprehension_questions.question: What is Sonal attending on Saturday?
options: A wedding
Her cousin's engagement
A birthday party
A college reunion

comprehension_questions.question: When are Sonal's parents arriving?
options: Saturday morning
Friday night
Sunday afternoon
Saturday evening

comprehension_questions.question: What time are Kavya and Meera meeting at the exhibition?
options: 10 AM
11 AM
12 PM
2 PM

sentences.wrong: I am meet my manager at 3 PM tomorrow.
sentences.correct: I am meeting my manager at 3 PM tomorrow.
sentences.explanation: Present continuous = am/is/are + verb-ING. 'Meet' must become 'meeting'.

sentences.wrong: Are you come to the party on Friday?
sentences.correct: Are you coming to the party on Friday?

sentences.wrong: She is not attend the training next week.
sentences.correct: She is not attending the training next week.

content.scenario: Your manager asks if you can join a meeting on Tuesday at 2 PM. You already have a confirmed dentist appointment at that exact time. What do you say?
options: I will go to the dentist maybe on Tuesday.
I'm seeing my dentist at 2 PM on Tuesday â€” I already have that appointment. Can we do a different time?
I don't come on Tuesday.
I see the dentist on Tuesday.
content.correct: I'm seeing my dentist at 2 PM on Tuesday â€” I already have that appointment. Can we do a different time?
content.explanation: A booked appointment = present continuous for future. The response is polite and suggests an alternative.

content.instruction: Talk about your real or imagined plans for this coming weekend. Use the present continuous for future and future time expressions.
content.prompt: What are you doing this weekend? Describe at least 4 arranged plans using present continuous.
content.example_response: This weekend is quite busy for me! On Saturday morning, I'm going to the gym with my friend Ravi â€” we've been planning it since last week. In the afternoon, I'm helping my mother shop for the upcoming festival. She wants to buy new clothes and some decoration items, so we're going to T. Nagar. On Saturday evening, I'm watching a movie with my cousins â€” we've already bought the tickets online for the 7 PM show. On Sunday, I'm not doing anything very special in the morning, just relaxing at home. But in the afternoon, I'm meeting my college friend Ananya for lunch at a restaurant near my place. We're catching up after almost three months.

content.instruction: Imagine you are a very busy person next week. Describe your week using present continuous for future plans â€” at least one plan per day.
content.prompt: Describe a busy week ahead using present continuous. Include: who you are meeting, where you are going, and what you are doing each day.
content.example_response: Next week is going to be very hectic! On Monday, I'm presenting the monthly report to my manager at 10 AM. I've already prepared the slides. On Tuesday, I'm having lunch with a client from Mumbai who is visiting our Bangalore office. On Wednesday, I'm attending a digital marketing workshop all day â€” my company has enrolled me. Thursday is slightly free â€” I'm only having a team check-in at 4 PM. But on Friday, I'm flying to Chennai for a family event. My cousin is getting married on Saturday. My flight is at 6 AM, so I'm waking up at 4! On Saturday afternoon, I'm attending the wedding ceremony, and on Sunday, I'm coming back to Bangalore in the evening.

content.instruction: Complete Priya's message to her friend using present continuous for future.
content.text:
Hi Meera!

Just wanted to update you on my plans. I ___ (1/not work) this Friday â€” I took a leave. On Friday morning, I ___ (2/go) to the art gallery with my sister. We already bought the tickets! In the evening, I ___ (3/join) the family for dinner at Grandma's house. On Saturday, my friend Riya ___ (4/visit) from Hyderabad â€” she ___ (5/stay) with us for two days. We ___ (6/take) her to some good restaurants in Chennai since it's her first visit. Are you free on Sunday? What ___ you ___ (7/do)?

content.title: Lesson 1 complete â€” Present continuous for future
key_points: Use am/is/are + -ing for FIXED, ARRANGED future plans
Always use a future time expression: tonight, tomorrow, next week, on Friday
Negative: I'm not / She isn't + verb-ing
This tense shows the plan is confirmed â€” not just a thought or prediction
content.next_lesson: Going to â€” review and deepen
